ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa zaŋ Reechɛl niŋ o paɣa, ka yur’ o gari Liya; ka daa lan tum tuma yuma ayopɔin ti Leeban. Yaakubu ni dɔɣi bi’ shɛba
translated: And Jacob took Rachel as his wife, and loved her more than Leah; and he served Laban for another seven years. Jacob had children

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: ka daa zaŋ bindir’ nyaɣisili maa min’ o ni mali bɔrɔbɔro shɛli ti o bia Yaakubu.
translated: and he gave the delicious food and the bread he had to his son Jacob.

ref: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
text: Ka Yisahaku yɛli Yaakubu, “M bia, saɣisim’ mirina ka n shih’ a nya a shiri nyɛla m bia Iso bee a pa o.”
translated: And Isaac said to Jacob, "My son, come closer so that I may feel you to see if you are truly my son Esau or not."

ref: The two servant women, Zilpah and Bilhah, together with their children went first, followed by Leah and her children, then by Rachel and Joseph.
text: O daa zaŋla dabipaɣiba ayi maa mini bɛ bihi bahi tooni, ka chɛ ka Liya min’ o bihi paya, ka chɛ ka Reechɛl mini Yisifu be nyaaŋa.
translated: He put the two female slaves and their children in front, and had Leah and her children follow, and had Rachel and Joseph be in the rear.

ref: Then the two servant women and their children came and bowed down to Esau.
text: Di saha ka dabipaɣiba maa mini bɛ bihi daa kana ti gban’ o tiŋa,
translated: At that time, the women and their children came to visit her in her hometown.
